settings.samplePathPatternDescription: Template for the file path of each sample. Required variables: {projectId}, {classId}, {fileName}.

hero.title: Teach your computer to see
hero.description:
Easy as growing a plant. Collect photos, train in seconds, and predict.
No code, no cloud.

install.title: Easy setup
install.description: Install and start using it right away — no Python, no terminal.

offline.title: No internet required
offline.description: Everything runs offline. Your data stays on your device.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-platform
crossPlatform.description: Runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

fast.title: Fast training
fast.description: Get results in seconds with just a few dozen images. Uses minimal CPU and memory.

unlimited.title: Apps within the app
unlimited.description: Each project is its own app. The number of projects is only limited by your disk space.

defaults.title: Smart defaults
defaults.description: Works great out of the box. Easily customize epochs, batch size, and more when needed.

flexibleInput.title: Easy data collection
flexibleInput.description: Capture images directly from your camera or use existing images.

easyPlay.title: Interactive testing
easyPlay.description: Test your trained model instantly with a simple, easy-to-use interface.

easyShare.title: Easy sharing
easyShare.description: Export your trained model for use in other apps or share your project across devices.

intro.headline: Train a computer to recognize anything you point your camera at.
intro.tagline: A fast, fun way to experience machine learning — no coding, no cloud, no expertise required.

howItWorks.title: Cool, how do I use it?

label.description: Collect photos of each thing you want to recognize — snap them with your camera or add images you already have.

train.description: Let the machine learn from what you collected — no code, no internet, no waiting.

play.description: Point your camera or drop a photo and watch it recognize things in real time.

liveDemo.title: See machine learning in action
liveDemo.description: Train a model with your own examples and see results instantly — visual, and easy to use.

cta.title: Turn a small spark of curiosity into something your computer can actually learn.
cta.description: Pick a simple idea, gather a few examples, and let PlantML turn it into a playful machine learning experiment in minutes.

create.name: What your project does. E.g. "Dog vs Cat", "Fruit Identifier".
create.description: In depth description of your project. Markdown supported.
create.icon: An icon that represents your project.

project.confirmDelete: Type "${name}" to confirm deletion. This will remove the all project data and cannot be undone.

directory.createErrorFallback: Unknown error while creating the project.
directory.emptyDescription: Create the first project to start collecting labels and samples.

label.title: Collect
label.noImages: No images in this class yet.

deleteClass.title: Delete class?
deleteClass.description: Delete "{{name}}" and all of its samples? This cannot be undone.

sampleGrid.sortByIdTooltip: Sort by date — restore original capture order

addImages.title: Add images for each class you want to recognize
addImages.subtitle: Variety within each class helps the model generalize to images it has never seen.
addImages.guide1: Mix angles, backgrounds, and lighting — avoid repetitive shots
addImages.guide2: Keep subjects sharp and centered — model input is 224×224px
addImages.guide3: Keep image counts balanced across all classes
addImages.tip: Capture in conditions similar to where the model will actually run.

play.title: Demo
play.settingsDescription: These settings apply to this project demo page.
play.description: Configure how people will experience {{name}}, then launch the project demo page.

form.modeUploadDescription: Upload an image to get a prediction
form.modeLiveDescription: Use your camera for real-time predictions
form.autoPredictOnUploadDescription: Automatically run predictions after uploading an image or video
form.showConfidenceScoresDescription: Display how confident the model is for each prediction
form.showAllClassesDescription: Show predictions for all classes instead of only the top results
form.topKDescription: Number of highest-confidence predictions to display
form.confidenceThresholdDescription: Only show predictions with confidence above this value
form.liveAspectRatioDescription: Frame shape for the live camera view
form.livePredictIntervalDescription: How often the model runs a prediction on the live feed

demo.noModel: This project does not have a trained model yet.
demo.dropzone: Drop an image here, or click to browse from your device.
demo.confidenceDisabled: Confidence display is disabled for this demo mode.

errors.encodeCurrentFrame: Could not encode the current camera frame.

train.title: Train
train.notReadyAlert: Not enough data yet. Complete the minimum class and sample requirements first.

detail.tfjs: Initialize runtime and select the execution backend.
detail.split: Split each class into train and validation sets automatically.
detail.embeddings: Convert images into 1280-d MobileNet feature vectors.
detail.head: Create a small dense classifier on top of the extracted features.
detail.trainingProgress: Optimize the classifier head for {{current}}/{{total}} epochs.
detail.trainingDefault: Optimize the classifier head on top of frozen MobileNet features.
detail.saving: Write the trained model artifacts to the project workspace.

export.title: Export Model
export.successTitle: Downloaded — unzip and open index.html, or drop the folder on Netlify.
export.deployNetlify: Drag the unzipped folder into app.netlify.com/drop — done.
export.deployLocal: Locally: npx serve . then open localhost:3000.
export.embedHint: Then load your model with tf.loadLayersModel("model.json").

faq.a: **PlantML** is a desktop app that lets you create and train image recognition models using your own data — quickly, simply, and entirely on your device.

Educators, students, creators, and makers of all kinds — anyone with an idea they want to explore.

*No machine learning experience required*.

faq.q: What can I train with it?
faq.a:
You can train image classification models using:

- Photos captured from your camera  
- Images uploaded from your device  

More input types may be supported in the future.

faq.q: Why isn’t my model working as expected?
faq.a:
Machine learning models depend heavily on the data you provide. If results aren’t what you expect, try:

- **Changing environments**  
  Train with different backgrounds, lighting conditions, and angles.

- **Improving data quality**  
  Use clear, consistent images for each class.

- **Balancing your dataset**  
  Make sure each class has a similar number of examples.

- **Avoiding confusing examples**  
  If two classes look similar, the model may struggle to distinguish them.

Experimenting and iterating is part of the process — small changes in your data can make a big difference.

faq.q: How many images do I need?
faq.a:
You can start with as few as 10 images per class.

More data usually leads to better results, especially if your images vary in lighting, angles, and backgrounds.

faq.q: What happens after I train a model?
faq.a:
You can immediately test your model within the app using:

- Image uploads  
- Live camera input  

You can also export your trained model for use in other applications.

faq.q: Can I use my model outside PlantML?
faq.a:
Yes.

You can export your model (for example, in TensorFlow.js format) and use it in your own apps, websites, or projects.

faq.q: Does it require internet or an account?
faq.a:
No.

PlantML runs entirely offline.

No sign-up, no cloud, no data uploads — everything stays on your device.
